title: How long did it take you to build and stabilize your algorithmic trading system?
author: Mindless_Routine_721
selftext:
레딧 글 초안을 작성해드릴게요:

I've built an AI-powered algorithmic trading system and got it running. But here's the thing - I believe the real challenge isn't just building it, but actually stabilizing the logic through live trading and continuous refinements.

I'm curious about everyone's experience.

How long did it take you to stabilize your system to the point where it consistently generates reasonable profits? 

What was your journey like from initial deployment to a stable, profitable system?

Would love to hear about:
- Your timeline from launch to stability
- Major issues you encountered during live trading
- How many iterations/refinements it took
- When you felt confident the system was truly stable

comments.author: YOLO401Kto0
comments.body:
Hey, I get this completely. I actually started back in 2018. My first algo looked perfect in backtests then went live and got wrecked af. Slippage, weird spreads, API hiccups — nothing worked like the simulation 😔🥀

So from 2019-2021 it was a shit ton grind starting new projects, failing, tweaking, overfitting issues, small wins and lots of losses. I learned fast that backtests lie, and live trading exposes every little flaw. Logging trades, handling edge cases, and making tiny improvements slowly became my focus. 

Then by 2022 my main algo finally started working consistently. It’s not perfect, but it survives live trading and produces steady results. Took me about four years to get here, but all the failures along the way taught me more than I could have imagined!

replies.author: Mindless_Routine_721
replies.body:
Thanks for sharing this - really appreciate the transparency. I’m running into the same things where live trading exposes flaws the backtest never showed.
I’m curious: when your system finally started stabilizing, what actually improved? Was it win rate, risk/reward, drawdown control, or just overall consistency?

replies.author: YOLO401Kto0
replies.body: Overall consistency and risk management. Those were the biggest fixes I had to come up with.
